channel.title: Seafood on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Seafood on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Rawbar by Domodomo
item.description: Summary Close to NYU, this is next door to its parent restaurant Domodomo. This is primarily a rawbar, meaning somewhat affordable raw seafood. The iced green tea is nice for a warm day. The green tea is quite flavorful, but of course if you want it to be sweeter, you can ask for sugar. We ordered a dozen oysters. Essentially 2 of the following: Half Moon (Cape Cod Bay, MA) : sweet, medium salnity, clean finish Wellfleet (Wellfleet Harbor, MA) : briny, creamy, high salinity Savage Harbour (PEI, Canada) : crisp, sweet, medium salinity Chekbooktook (New Brunswick) : buttery, sweet, delicate Duxbury (Duxbury Bay, MA) : briny, sweet, and buttery finish the daily oysters : can't remember what the waiter said about these other than being the saltiest of the east coast They serve oysters with your choice of 2 condiments: cocktail sauce, mignonette, wasabi lime sorbet, thai chili, and ponzu.

item.title: The Fulton
item.description: Summary This place is one of Jean Georges’ new seafood-centric restaurant situated right at the corner of the Pier near South Street Seaport. This area has a tiny Boston like mall where people can shop and eat at some decent restaurants. The iPic theater is also here too. The area is largely going through some sort of construction. The restaurant is located at the edge of the pier and overlooks the water facing towards the Brooklyn Bridge.

item.title: Taverna Kyclades
item.description: Summary We came here around 8:30 PM on a rather cold Saturday evening. Typically there is a long line outside, but today people were crowded and bundled up inside. I put my name down for 2. The wait was approximately 40-50 minutes. We had contemplated leaving when we heard the wait time, but we decided to power through and wait. It was worth it. The greek salad was pretty straight forward but large.

item.title: Grand Central Oyster Bar
item.description: Pretty decent. Worth this 1 time trip and that's about it. The oyster pan roast was alright. The soft shell crab was pretty decent too. I think I'd get the clam chowder over the oyster pan roast.

item.title: Simply Hooked
item.description: This used to be in the Lower East Side, but I guess they set up shop in this Gansevoort Market area. It wasn't that good. I prefer the A Salt & Battery place instead.

item.title: Hot N Juicy Crawfish
item.description: Got the soft shell poboy. Was decent for lunch. $17 for fries, drink, and a poboy. The normal stuff is rather expensive. Probably a $2-3 per pound markup compared to LA.

item.title: Lobster Press
item.description: This is Marc Forgione's fast food-ish restaurant near Penn Station. I got the chili lobster here, but it's not as good as having it at the restaurant. It's also CLOSED.

item.title: Greenpoint Fish and Lobster
item.description: Surprisingly good. The oyster shuckers did a good job keeping it juicy. Happy hour is cheap!

item.title: Flora Bar
item.description: Seafood centric. Ambience is good, but the food is eh.

item.title: Zadie's Oyster Room
item.description: Different cooking styles for all types of oysters. Was pretty good.

item.title: Cape House
item.description: Scallops and clam strips were good. Pickles were salty not sour.

item.title: Sunken Hundred
item.description: Good restaurant. I like the spanish octopus, fish churros, and pork chop. Unfortunately, it's CLOSED.
